title: Badminton's 3x15 Scoring System to Debut in Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This new scoring system will be implemented across all domestic ranking tournaments and National Championships for U-13, U-15, and U-17 categories in India, starting at Ernakulam.
summary: India's domestic badminton circuit will adopt a new 3x15 scoring system for junior categories, aiming to enhance competitive play and strategy.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes Runner-Up at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open, showcasing strong form before ultimately faltering at the last step against Su Li Yang.
summary: Indian star Kidambi Srikanth's impressive run at the US Open concluded with a loss in the final, marking a significant performance in the Super 300 event.

title: Rounak Chouhan Upsets World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan delivered a stunning upset by defeating World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open, highlighting his potential.
summary: Rounak Chouhan, an emerging Indian talent, made headlines at the US Open by beating a top-ranked opponent, signaling a promising future.

title: BAI Issues Addendum for Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships Selection
indian_angle: Badminton Association of India (BAI) has released further criteria for selecting Indian players for the upcoming Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships 2026.
summary: New guidelines have been issued by BAI to clarify the selection process for India's junior squad participating in the continental championships.

title: BAI Warns Against Unauthorized Badminton Leagues and Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has issued an advisory cautioning players and officials against participating in unauthorized badminton leagues and tournaments.
summary: BAI has released a stern advisory to protect the integrity of the sport and its players from unapproved events, including specific warnings about the Badminton Premier League.

title: Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Tournament Scheduled for Chennai
indian_angle: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 is set to take place in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16th to 23rd, 2026.
summary: Chennai will host a key national ranking tournament next month, providing Indian senior players an opportunity to improve their standings.
